queue.task: Review yesterday (500+ tasks across 3 sessions). Assess quality, pick Sunday focus: depth over breadth
queue.summary: Reviewed yesterday: 530 tasks, 72% EXPLORE breadth spiral. Today queue is well-structured for depth: CI fix, VM callbacks, fuzzer gap. No queue changes needed.

queue.goal: Fix monkey-lang GitHub token workflow scope + CI pipeline
queue.summary: Planned CI fix: copy workflow to repo root, push with workflow scope. Single BUILD task.

queue.task: Copy CI workflow to repo root .github/workflows/, push with workflow scope token, verify CI runs
queue.summary: CI workflow added to repo root, pushed, GitHub Actions running green (Node 18/20/22). Urgent TODO cleared.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, dashboard health, email check
queue.summary: Git pushed, dashboard healthy, GH notifications checked (5 items, none urgent)

queue.task: Reflect on CI fix. Assess monkey-lang SSA pipeline - real but unused by codegen. Worth wiring?
queue.summary: SSA pipeline real but low-impact vs VM callbacks (19x perf gap). Keep queue order: VM callbacks first, SSA→DCE later.

queue.goal: VM callback mechanism for native HOF builtins (prelude 19x slower than native)
queue.summary: Planned VM callback: callClosureSync method + native map/filter/reduce builtins. 2 BUILD tasks.

queue.task: Implement callClosureSync in VM + native map/filter/reduce builtins
queue.summary: VM callback mechanism implemented. callClosureSync enables native HOF builtins. map 4x faster, filter 3.9x, reduce 1.5x. 1149/1149 tests passing.

queue.task: Benchmark native vs prelude HOFs, add tests
queue.summary: Added 21 native HOF tests (edge cases: nested, closures, recursion, chaining) + bench-hof.js. All pass.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, dashboard, notifications
queue.summary: Git pushed (both repos), CI running, dashboard ok.

queue.task: Mid-day reflection. Review VM callback work. What next for HenryDB fuzzer gap?
queue.summary: VM callback was a clean win. HenryDB fuzzer gap: 1.4% from type affinity (3 fixes identified in scratch notes). Queue order good.

queue.goal: HenryDB: close fuzzer gap - mixed type comparisons and UNION type affinity
queue.summary: Planned 2 BUILD tasks: (1) sqliteCompare for WHERE, (2) sqliteCompare for UNION ORDER BY. Targeting 98.6% → 99%+.

queue.task: Add sqliteCompare function + use in WHERE COMPARE (LT/GT/LE/GE)
queue.summary: Added sqliteCompare (NULL<num<text<blob). Updated WHERE COMPARE (LT/GT/LE/GE), BETWEEN, 4x ORDER BY sites, set-ops. Fixed fuzzer harness parseFloat bug. All fuzzer tests pass.

queue.task: Use sqliteCompare in UNION/set-ops ORDER BY + run diff fuzzer to measure improvement
queue.summary: Set-ops already updated in T11. Verified: mixed type comparisons match SQLite (int < string). All 84 diff fuzzer tests pass.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, dashboard, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Pushed workspace and HenryDB. Cleaned TODO (removed completed VM callback and fuzzer gap items).

queue.task: Afternoon direction. Dead code elimination via SSA or HenryDB COUNT(DISTINCT)?
queue.summary: SSA→DCE is the right call. Depth check passed (3/3 yes). SSA is genuine compiler engineering, builds on existing infra.

queue.goal: Per-function SSA enabling dead code elimination in monkey-lang compiler
queue.summary: Plan: 3 tasks — AST-level DCE first (unreachable + constant folding), wire into pipeline, then SSA-level if time.

queue.task: Implement AST-level DCE pass (unreachable after return + constant branch folding)
queue.summary: DCE module enhanced with eliminateDeadCode wrapper. Not wired into compiler (compiler already handles constant folding). Learning: DCE as compiler pre-pass conflicts with compilers own inline handling.

queue.task: Wire DCE into compilation pipeline + add tests
queue.summary: Already addressed in T15. DCE wired via pipeline.js (existing). Direct compiler integration causes if-false conflicts. Tested, 1170/1170 pass.

queue.task: Explore SSA-level DCE (mark dead defs via def-use chains, annotate AST)
queue.summary: SSA-level DCE analysis built. Def-use chains from SSA form identify dead definitions. Handles complex expressions via base name matching. 9 new tests. Conservative approach preserves side-effectful assignments.

queue.task: Housekeeping: commit, push, dashboard
queue.summary: Git pushed, workspace committed.

queue.task: Deep dive: LuaJIT allocation sinking paper - implications for monkey-lang escape analysis
queue.summary: LuaJIT allocation sinking explored. Key findings: (1) sinking is JIT-only, not applicable to bytecode interpreters (2) monkey-lang escape analysis is correct but conservative — intermediate arrays from map→reduce chains marked escaping (3) interprocedural analysis could help but complex. 3/4 hypotheses confirmed.

queue.task: HenryDB query optimizer: cost model unification across 6 execution engines
queue.summary: Cost model exploration: 4 engines, only volcano uses PG-style cost model. Adaptive engine uses heuristic thresholds + runtime feedback. Unification gap is real but runtime feedback corrects wrong initial choices. 2/4 hypotheses confirmed.

queue.task: Design monkey-lang class syntax: methods, inheritance, self keyword, constructor
queue.summary: Class design already exists in scratch/monkey-lang-class-design.md. Option A: compile to closures. Explicit self, init constructor, extends+super. ~200 LOC total. Good for today.

queue.goal: Implement monkey-lang class syntax (parser + compiler + VM support)
queue.summary: Plan: 3 tasks — (1) lexer/parser/AST, (2) compiler (closure factory), (3) tests

queue.task: Add CLASS token + ClassStatement AST node + parser support
queue.summary: Parser done: CLASS/EXTENDS/SUPER tokens, ClassStatement + SuperExpression AST, parseClassStatement, dotted set assignment. 1179 tests pass.

queue.task: Compile ClassStatement to closure factory function (Option A)
queue.summary: Class compilation complete. ClassStatement → factory fn + method let-bindings. New OpSetIndex opcode for property mutation. Both speak(rex) and rex.speak() work. 1179/1179 tests pass.

queue.task: Add class tests (basic, inheritance, methods, super calls)
queue.summary: 15 class tests added — basic, methods, mutation, edge cases. All 1194 tests pass.

queue.task: Choose next depth project: HenryDB COUNT(DISTINCT) bug vs monkey-lang inheritance vs WASM Phase 1
queue.summary: Inheritance first (completes class feature, 30-60 min), then WASM Phase 1 if time. Both are depth work.

queue.goal: Add class inheritance (extends + super) to monkey-lang
queue.summary: Plan: 2 tasks — compile inheritance + test it

queue.task: Compile class inheritance: extends copies parent methods, super.method() calls parent
queue.summary: Inheritance works! extends for method sharing, super.init() for parent constructor calls. Method override replaces parent method in scope. 1194/1194 tests pass.

queue.task: Test inheritance: basic extends, method override, super calls
queue.summary: 5 inheritance tests added (inherit, override, super.init, 3-level chain, child new methods). 1199 total tests, all pass.

queue.goal: WASM compiler Phase 1: integer + function compilation
queue.summary: WASM Phase 1 plan: 3 tasks — binary encoder, AST→WASM compiler, tests with Node runtime verification

queue.task: Implement WASM binary encoder (LEB128, sections, module structure)
queue.summary: WASM encoder built. Valid binary: 41-byte add(a,b) module runs in Node WASM runtime. LEB128 + sections + module builder.

queue.task: Compile monkey-lang integer expressions + functions to WASM
queue.summary: WASM compiler Phase 1 complete! Compiles integer arithmetic + functions + if/else + comparisons. fib(30): 7ms WASM vs 892ms VM = 120x speedup.

queue.task: Add WASM tests: arithmetic, function calls, verify with Node WASM runtime
queue.summary: 26 WASM tests covering arithmetic, comparisons, if/else, locals, function calls, recursion (fib/fact), return, main expression. 1225 total tests, all pass.

queue.task: Update TODO.md, write session learnings scratch note, clean up
queue.summary: TODO updated (removed completed items, added new). Session learnings scratch note written (VM callbacks, WASM, classes, SQLite affinity, SSA, DCE).

queue.task: Plan next depth work: HenryDB COUNT(DISTINCT) multi-join bug investigation
queue.summary: Investigated COUNT(DISTINCT) multi-join: all tested scenarios match SQLite. Bug may have been fixed previously or was very specific. Tested: 2-way join, 3-way join, GROUP BY, HAVING, NULL values, duplicate products. All correct.
Bug not reproducible — skipped

queue.task: WASM Phase 1.5: Add while loops + set statements to WASM compiler
queue.summary: WASM while loops + set statements. Iterative fib: 41ns/call (native perf). 5 new tests. 1230 total tests.

queue.task: WASM: Add for..range compilation
queue.summary: WASM for-loop compilation. C-style for→block/loop. sum_range and power tests. 1232 total tests.

queue.task: [DEPTH PIVOT] Full test sweep of monkey-lang
queue.summary: Full monkey-lang sweep: 1232/1232 tests pass. Zero failures. Clean.

queue.task: [DEPTH PIVOT] Stress-test WASM compiler edge cases - find bugs
queue.summary: WASM stress test: 1 bug found (empty fn body crash) and fixed. 5 edge cases tested: deep nesting, forward refs, i32 max, overflow all OK. 20% insight rate.

queue.task: [DEPTH PIVOT] Stress-test class compilation edge cases - find bugs
queue.summary: Class stress test: 1 known limitation confirmed (method name collision between classes). Other hypotheses all passed: 3-level super.init works, no-init methods work, instance isolation correct, method→method calls work.

queue.task: [DEPTH PIVOT] Full test sweep of HenryDB
queue.summary: HenryDB sweep: 4310/4310 pass, 3 skipped, 0 failures. Clean.

queue.task: Design fix for class method collision: hash-based dispatch vs name mangling
queue.summary: Decision: Store methods on instance hash + change parseMethodCall to index-based dispatch + VM fallback for builtins. Most correct solution.

queue.goal: Fix class method collision via hash-based method dispatch
queue.summary: 3 tasks planned

queue.task: Store class methods as closures on instance hash in _compileClass
queue.summary: Class methods now have mangled names (ClassName__method) for collision avoidance + unmangled aliases for dot-dispatch. 1232 tests pass. Dot-dispatch still has last-wins limitation.

queue.task: Change parseMethodCall to index-based dispatch + VM builtin fallback
queue.summary: Compile-time method dispatch: dot-calls resolve to symbols/builtins at compile time. arr.len() works (builtin), rex.speak() works (class method). Collision still exists for dot-syntax but Cat__speak(cat) works for explicit dispatch. 1232 tests pass.

queue.task: Update tests for hash-based method dispatch
queue.summary: 4 method dispatch tests added (class dot, builtin array/string, mangled collision avoidance). 1236 total tests.

queue.task: Build a real monkey-lang program using classes + WASM: benchmark project (Mandelbrot set computation)
queue.summary: Mandelbrot benchmark: WASM vs VM. Results match. 330x speedup (0.7ms vs 231ms for 80x60 grid). 301-byte WASM binary. Fixed i32 overflow issue with x100 scale.

queue.task: Update scratch/INDEX.md with new session learnings, update CURRENT.md
queue.summary: Updated scratch/INDEX.md and CURRENT.md with session progress.

queue.task: Design WASM i64 support for monkey-lang (BigInt integration, 64-bit arithmetic)
queue.summary: i64 design: change all types to i64, comparisons produce i32 (unchanged control flow). BigInt on JS boundary. Simple mechanical change.

queue.task: Add WASM i64 compilation support (64-bit integers)
queue.summary: WASM i64 support added. useI64 option switches all types to 64-bit. Handles 10 billion+ without overflow. Mandelbrot x1000 precision works (0.72ms). 1236 tests pass.

queue.task: Re-run Mandelbrot at x1000 precision with i64 WASM
queue.summary: Already done in T53. i64 Mandelbrot at x1000: 146861 total iterations, 0.72ms.

queue.task: Mid-session compaction: flush daily log, update CURRENT.md, git push all
queue.summary: Compacted. All repos pushed. CURRENT.md updated. 32 tasks done.

queue.task: Add i64 WASM tests + update benchmark to include i64
queue.summary: 6 i64 WASM tests: large multiply (10B), overflow, fibonacci, sum to 1M (500B). 1240 total tests.

queue.task: Investigate HenryDB PL/SQL → procedure handler wiring (854 LOC exists)
queue.summary: PL/SQL code exists: 854 LOC parser+interpreter in projects/henrydb/. Has tests. Wire-up needed: (1) copy plsql.js to src/ (2) modify callUserFunction to use PLInterpreter for plsql language (3) detect DECLARE/BEGIN in function bodies.

queue.task: Wire PL/SQL into HenryDB: copy plsql.js, update callUserFunction
queue.summary: PL/SQL wired into HenryDB! factorial(5)=120, classify() with IF/ELSIF, SELECT INTO works. 854 LOC PL/SQL engine integrated. 4313 tests pass.

queue.task: Add PL/SQL integration tests to main HenryDB test suite
queue.summary: 8 PL/SQL integration tests added. Fixed unary minus bug in expression evaluator. Fixed auto-detected PL/SQL language assignment. All tests pass.

queue.task: Stress-test PL/SQL: edge cases, error handling, nested blocks
queue.summary: PL/SQL stress test: nested IF ✅, FOR loop ✅. BUGS: recursive functions return NaN (expression evaluator cant handle function calls), PL→PL calls return string (same root cause). Expression evaluator is string-based regex, needs enhancement for function calls.

queue.task: Add FOR loop support to PL/SQL integration (FOR i IN 1..10 LOOP)
queue.summary: FOR loops already work (confirmed in EXPLORE). Skipping.

queue.task: Fix PL/SQL expression evaluator: handle function calls in expressions
queue.summary: Fixed PL/SQL expression evaluator for function calls. Recursive PL/SQL (rec_fact(5)=120) and nested PL→PL (inc(inc(40))=42) now work. 11/11 PL/SQL tests pass.

queue.task: Add monkey-lang REPL command --wasm to compile and run WASM
queue.summary: Added --engine=wasm to REPL with --wasm-type=i32|i64|f64 support. File execution and interactive modes both work. All existing engines unchanged.

queue.task: WASM: Add import support for linking multiple WASM modules
queue.summary: Added WASM import section to wasm.js builder (addImport method, import section encoding). WasmCompiler processes Monkey import statements into WASM imports with importSignatures option. 5 new tests all passing. Backward compatible.

queue.task: Session retrospective: what worked, what to improve, tomorrow focus
queue.summary: Retrospective: WASM compiler biggest win (130-330x). Queue ID confusion after compaction. Tomorrow: OpMethodCall + WASM strings + SSA DCE wiring.

queue.task: Update MEMORY.md with todays significant events and learnings
queue.summary: MEMORY.md updated with exploration results, VM perf stats, GC/TCO/SCCP findings, tomorrow focus.

queue.task: Enhanced benchmark: compare i32/i64/f64/VM across fibonacci, Mandelbrot, and iterative algorithms
queue.summary: Built comprehensive benchmark-wasm.js comparing VM vs WASM (i32/i64/f64) across 6 algorithms. WASM is 14-800x faster. i64 slightly faster than i32 for iteration, i32 better for recursion, f64 consistently slowest WASM variant.

queue.task: Update monkey-lang README with WASM compilation docs and benchmark results
queue.summary: Updated README with comprehensive WASM benchmarks (6 algorithms, 3 types), REPL --engine=wasm docs, import support docs. Updated test count to 1,250.

queue.task: [DEPTH PIVOT 2] Final test sweeps + evening summary
queue.summary: Fixed 3 WASM f64 bugs: missing opcodes, broken constant encoding (1 vs 8 bytes), wrong comparison op names. All 1245 tests pass.

queue.task: Investigate monkey-lang constant propagation (SCCP) accuracy with complex programs
queue.summary: SCCP bug found: propagateConstants uses toSSA which gives empty results for function bodies. Needs perFunctionSSA. SCCP is currently useless for real programs.

queue.task: Design runtime method dispatch for class polymorphism (OpMethodCall)
queue.summary: Design refined: 3-phase implementation (instance methods → shared prototype → inline caching). Phase 1 is ~100 LOC, sufficient for correctness.

queue.task: Write scratch note: runtime dispatch implementation notes for tomorrow
queue.summary: Already completed during T71 — scratch/runtime-dispatch-plan.md has full implementation notes.

queue.task: Stress-test WASM compiler with edge cases: empty functions, deeply nested ifs, large constants, 0-arg functions
queue.summary: [auto-resolved: orphaned in-progress task]

queue.task: HenryDB: run differential fuzzer, analyze remaining failure patterns
queue.summary: Ran differential fuzzer: 99.3% pass rate (993/1000). 7 failures all trace to type affinity in comparisons — UNION dedup, window ORDER BY, and subquery AVG comparison dont use sqliteCompare. Documented in scratch note.

queue.task: Design WASM Phase 2: string support via linear memory
queue.summary: Reviewed projects-level wasm-compiler string approach: linear memory + data segments + host imports for str ops. Root-level compiler should add: memory section, data section, string host imports. Alternatively, promote projects-level compiler as canonical. Recommend extending root-level for pedagogical clarity.

queue.task: Update monkey-lang stats in README (test count, LOC)
queue.summary: Updated README stats: 24.8K LOC, 91 files, 1250 tests, 2200+ commits. Pushed.

queue.task: Test HenryDB PL/SQL with complex programs (cursors, exception handling, bulk operations)
queue.summary: PL/HenryDB tested: DECLARE+IF/ELSIF+WHILE+FOR+SELECT INTO+EXCEPTION all work. Missing: CASE statement (parser crash), cursors, DML inside PL blocks (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE not recognized), PERFORM, NOTICE with format. Core procedural logic is solid.

queue.task: Analyze monkey-lang optimizer pipeline: what optimizations actually fire on real programs?
queue.summary: Pipeline has 7 passes (TypeCheck,DCE,CFG,SSA,ConstProp,Liveness,Escape) but only DCE modifies code (constant branch elimination). Dead var detection works but doesnt remove. ConstProp counts but doesnt propagate. Escape analysis is purely informational. The pipeline is analysis-only, not transformational — real work would be: (1) DCE that removes dead let stmts, (2) ConstProp that replaces references.

queue.task: Design pipeline DCE: remove dead let statements from AST
queue.summary: Design: removeDeadLets(program, deadVars) in dce.js. Side-effect check: literals/fn-lits/identifiers safe to remove, function calls convert to ExpressionStatement. Wire into pipeline after findDeadVariables.

queue.goal: Wire dead variable removal into DCE pass + compiler pipeline
queue.summary: Plan: 2 BUILD tasks — (1) removeDeadLets with side-effect analysis, (2) wire into pipeline + tests

queue.task: Implement removeDeadLets in dce.js with side-effect analysis
queue.summary: Added removeDeadLets() and isSideEffectFree() to dce.js. Handles safe removal (literals, fn lits) and side-effect conversion (calls). All existing DCE tests pass.

queue.task: Wire removeDeadLets into pipeline, add tests for dead let removal
queue.summary: Wired removeDeadLets into pipeline. Pipeline now actually removes dead let statements. Fixed 3 existing tests, added 2 new tests. 15/15 pipeline tests passing.

queue.task: Implement AST-level dead let removal in DCE pass
queue.summary: Already completed as part of T87+T88. removeDeadLets implemented and wired into pipeline.

queue.task: Fix class method collision: hash-based dispatch on instance
queue.summary: Fixed class method collision with hash-based dispatch. Methods stored on instance hash, builtins still scope-dispatched. Inheritance chains resolve correctly (3-level). 24/24 class tests pass.

queue.task: Add super.method() support for non-init methods
queue.summary: super.method() already works for non-init methods — tested 3-level chain (Base→Mid→Leaf) with super.foo() chaining. The compiler resolves super.method() using _currentSuperClass + mangled names.

queue.task: WASM: Add memory section + data segment for string constants
queue.summary: Added WASM memory section + data segments to wasm.js. addMemory(), addDataSegment(), addStringConstant() with [i32 len][utf8] format. exportMemory(). 7 new tests all passing.

queue.task: Stress test class inheritance: diamond, 5-level chain, method override edge cases
queue.summary: Found and fixed prototype pollution bug in lexer KEYWORDS map (toString/constructor broken). Also verified: 5-level chain, super chain, two-branch inheritance, instance isolation, complex methods all work. 0 test regressions.

queue.task: Deep dive neural-net 38K LOC: RLHF, mixture of experts, vision transformer
queue.summary: Neural-net deep dive: MoE (forward/backward/update works, learns simple tasks), Transformer (PE, MHA, Encoder, Decoder, CrossAttn all work), 1337 tests all passing. Framework is solid with autograd, 20K LOC, 96 files. No RLHF class but has REINFORCE policy gradient.

queue.task: Fix arr[0]() syntax: index then immediate call
queue.summary: arr[0]() already works — tested arrays, hashes, nested arrays, all produce correct results. No fix needed.

queue.task: Add CASE statement to PL/HenryDB parser and interpreter
queue.summary: Added CASE statement to PL/HenryDB — simple CASE (CASE expr WHEN val) and searched CASE (CASE WHEN cond). Nested CASE, CASE in loops, CASE_NOT_FOUND error. All PL tests pass.

queue.task: Add DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) inside PL/HenryDB BEGIN blocks
queue.summary: Added INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE inside PL/HenryDB blocks. DML parsed as raw SQL with variable substitution. Works with loops, CASE, IF. All PL tests pass.

queue.task: WASM: Add global section for mutable global variables
queue.summary: Added WASM global section: addGlobal(), exportGlobal(), encodeF64/F32 helpers. Mutable globals work as counters/state. All WASM tests pass.

queue.task: Run full monkey-lang test suite, check for any new failures from recent changes
queue.summary: Full monkey-lang test suite: 1257 tests, 0 failures. All recent changes (class dispatch, lexer fix, DCE, WASM features) clean.

queue.task: WASM compiler: use globals for top-level let variables
queue.summary: WASM compiler now uses globals for top-level let variables. __init function initializes them, main calls __init first. Functions can close over globals. All WASM tests pass.

queue.task: Add cursor support to PL/HenryDB (DECLARE CURSOR, OPEN, FETCH, CLOSE)
queue.summary: Added cursor support to PL/HenryDB: DECLARE CURSOR FOR, OPEN/FETCH INTO/CLOSE, EXIT WHEN NOT FOUND, LOOP, RECORD type, record field access. All PL tests pass.

queue.task: Add WASM table section + indirect calls for higher-order functions
queue.summary: Added WASM table + element sections: addTable, addTableElement, call_indirect opcode, exportTable. Indirect function calls work correctly. All WASM tests pass.

queue.task: Run henrydb full test suite, check for regressions
queue.summary: PL tests all pass. SQL/joins pass. 1 pre-existing window function failure (running sum partition). No regressions from CASE/DML/cursor changes.

queue.task: WASM compiler: compile higher-order functions using table + call_indirect
queue.summary: WASM compiler now supports higher-order functions: functions as values via table indices, call_indirect for dynamic dispatch. apply(double, 21) → 42. All tests pass.

queue.task: Add PL/HenryDB stored functions: CREATE FUNCTION + call from SQL
queue.summary: Wired PL/HenryDB as stored function language: CREATE FUNCTION ... LANGUAGE plhenrydb. Functions callable from SQL (SELECT, WHERE). Parameters, DB access, all working. 38/38 tests pass.

queue.task: Monkey: add string interpolation syntax: f-strings or template literals
queue.summary: Template literals (backtick + dollar-brace) already implemented. Tested: string interpolation, expressions in templates, f-strings. All working.

queue.task: Fix HenryDB running sum window function partition bug
queue.summary: Window function test was wrong, not the engine. RANGE framing includes all tied ORDER BY rows. Fixed test expectations. Verified against SQLite.
